content:
Character: Leo
Perception: Leo has the look of a man who has given up on appearances not out of rebellion but out of genuine forgetfulness — rumpled flannel, crooked glasses, coffee stains. It's almost endearing. He's clearly intelligent; we had a brief exchange at the coffee shop about music and he referenced Gainsbourg, which surprised me. He's a programmer but carries himself like a poet who took a wrong turn. There's a sadness in him I recognize — the melancholy of someone living adjacent to the life they actually want. We're nodding acquaintances so far. I suspect he sees me as an overdressed European oddity.

He's a programmer but carries himself like a poet who took a wrong turn. There's a sadness in him I recognize — the melancholy of someone living adjacent to the life they actually want. We're nodding acquaintances so far. I suspect he sees me as an overdressed European oddity.
